title: Carapa granatum
intro: Carapa guianensis, commonly known as Carapa granatum or Crabwood, is a tropical tree species native to the Amazon and Guiana regions of South America. The health benefits of Carapa guianensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Carapa guianensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Carapa guianensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and immune function.
